content:
# Recommendations — next plan iter (after iter-100)

## TOP — build the shared infrastructure, do NOT re-prove the keystone
Both open residuals of (a) collapse to ONE obligation: the **`leftAdjointUniq`/`homEquiv`
unit-transpose carrier value** — the H1 pullback↔pushforward identification at `𝟙_X`, sectionwise
`= (f.appIso V).hom`. This is FLANK-scale infra (cf. the iter-098 leaf), NOT a fill.
- **Action:** dispatch an **effort-breaker** (or a dedicated infra `prove` lane) on the carrier value
  lemma, seeded by the two named obstacles in `forgetUnitRestrict_eq_pushforwardUnit` (RES1, L1566) and
  `pullbackUnit_sheafify_reconcile` ((*), L1607). Mathlib levers already identified in-code:
  `Adjunction.homEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app`, `Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app`, plus the
  `leftAdjointUniq`-counit compatibility. Template mirror: `presheafDualUnitIso_naturality`
  (DualInverse:317).
- **Do NOT** re-dispatch a plain prover at `dual_unit_iso_restrict_assemble` — it is already assembled
  (body sorry-free). Proving at it again would re-grind the same wall (085–087 failure mode).
- Once the carrier lemma lands, RES1 closes immediately and (*) closes by the same unfolding; then (a)
  is fully sorry-free and **(c) `trivialisation_restrict_compat` (L2451) unblocks** (the S4a telescope).

## HARD-GATE prerequisite before any prover at this chapter
The keystone chapter (`Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`) proof prose is now **STALE** (lvb MAJOR; my
`% NOTE:` flags it): it claims L1′ is the sole seam-crossing step, but L1′ is sorry-free and the open
work is RES1/RES2. **Dispatch a blueprint-writer** for this chapter to: (1) rewrite the
`lem:dual_unit_iso_restrict_assemble` proof prose to the assembly + RES1/RES2 decomposition; (2) author
blueprint blocks for the 2 substantive residuals (`\uses` them from the keystone proof). Then re-run
blueprint-reviewer (scoped fast-path) before the infra prover.

## Coverage debt — 4 new lean_aux with no blueprint (unmatched = 159)
Author blueprint entries (planner writes prose; I do not):
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.unit_assemble` (L1529) — generic abstract-`[Category]` outer
  assembler; uses only `Category.assoc`. (minor / generic plumbing)
- `…hcore_assemble` (L1542) — generic core assembler; uses only `Category.assoc`. (minor)
- `…forgetUnitRestrict_eq_pushforwardUnit` (L1566, RES1) — SUBSTANTIVE (open sorry); uses
  `pushforwardBetaUnitEpsAppOne`, `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`,
  `presheafPushforwardUnitIso`.
- `…pullbackUnit_sheafify_reconcile` (L1607, (*)) — SUBSTANTIVE (open sorry); uses `pullbackUnitIso`,
  `sheafificationCompPullback`, `presheafPullbackUnitIso`, sheafification counit.

## Comment cleanup (lean-auditor MAJOR ×2 + MINOR ×2 — non-blocking, schedule a refactor lane)
- TensorObjInverse L2254–2258: stale "BLOCKER for executing now" planning comment (already EXECUTED
  iter-066) — strike.
- TensorObjInverse L2723–2730: `exists_tensorObj_inverse` docstring cites iter-206/218/225/226
  "(current state)" — these exceed iter-100 and misrepresent project state; correct/strip.
- MINOR: docstring status-change-log L793–804; `unit_assemble` shadows outer `X`/`Y` (harmless).

## Deferred (unchanged rationale)
- Dead private `exists_tensorObj_inverse` dup `TensorObjSubstrate.lean:716` (the lone sorry there) —
  cleanup phase; deletion candidate.
- Helper-home decision (iter-099 auditor MAJOR): restore the 3 dropped DualInverse helpers upstream —
  refactor lane, post-recovery cleanup.

## Blocked — do not re-assign
- `trivialisation_restrict_compat` (L2451): structurally gated on (a) fully closing. Not a regression
  (sorry in v4.30). Hold until the carrier infra lands.
